trade.summary: Modest trade relationship. Brazil exports soybeans, coffee, iron ore, and seafood products. Norway exports offshore oil equipment, salmon, and maritime technology. The relationship is more significant for environmental cooperation than trade.

diplomatic.summary: Strong diplomatic ties especially around Amazon conservation. Norway is the largest single contributor to the Amazon Fund (having contributed over $1 billion). Under Lula, cooperation on Amazon protection has been highlighted as a flagship bilateral achievement.

military.summary: No military alliance. Very limited military cooperation. Norway is NATO member; Brazil is non-aligned.

financial.summary: Norway's sovereign wealth fund (GPFG) holds Brazilian equities. Equinor has some Brazilian offshore oil interests. Norwegian financial flows to Amazon Fund are the most significant bilateral financial feature.

energy.summary: Equinor (formerly Statoil) has offshore oil operations in Brazil. Norway provides technical expertise in offshore oil and subsea technology. Norway also funds renewable energy transition projects in Brazil.

scientific.summary: Strong cooperation in environmental science, tropical ecology, and ocean research. Norwegian research institutions partner with Brazilian universities. NORAD funds conservation and research programs in the Amazon.
